title: Column Color Shading
excerpt:
Hi Monday.com community! 
I have a very massive board and in the board several Columns are clustered together to capture certain data. I was just thinking that it would be super awesome :smiley: If we could (Select MULTI…

posts.author: alejencia (Alex Becerra)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Monday.com community!

I have a very massive board and in the board several Columns are clustered together to capture certain data. I was just thinking that it would be super awesome If we could (Select MULTIPLE Columns) and have a setting to color shade the columns … that way we can have a nice visual of the clusters / phases.

This is what I mean.

Current view …

image1539×445 50.2 KB

with the feature view …

image1537×519 51.9 KB

posts.author: kolinhayes (Kolin Hayes)
posts.cookedHtml: A subtle grey alternating each Pulse row would be helpful, too. On wider/larger screens it’s difficult to track what pulse I’m in unless I click it, close the update and the pulse stays highlighted… extra work.

posts.author: AshleyMcDonald (Ashley McDonald)
posts.cookedHtml: Agreed!!! Love the color shading idea!

posts.author: JustinJ (Justin Junioous)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @alejencia, @kolinhayes,

@AshleyMcDonald Welcome to the community! Please keep sharing your feedback.

Thank you all for taking the time to share your thoughts. I will be sure to pass this along to our team for review.

In the meantime, stay active and continue to share your feedback and different use cases that you use.

Cheers,
Justin

posts.author: alejencia (Alex Becerra)
posts.cookedHtml:
@JustinJ and the rest of the Monday.com team.

Is this part of future enhancements? if not please consider.

Thanks !

posts.author: kyrinfield (Kyrin Field)
posts.cookedHtml: I made a similar feature request, but for specific cell shading. I also endorse this

posts.author: Nadious (Mike McGuire)
posts.cookedHtml: This would also be super helpful for our boards. You got my vote.

posts.author: kyrinfield (Kyrin Field)
posts.cookedHtml: Still a major supporter of this and individual cell shading as well.

posts.author: Klink (Kalmia)
posts.cookedHtml: I would also love this! Thank you.

posts.author: Eltjo (Eltjo Smit)
posts.cookedHtml:
Monday is such an visually appealing tool to work with. This request combines visual and practical together to optimize board readability. Great idea.

Same goes for Footer of columns also a small enhancement and much readability improvements in boards when this feature is added to any footer type

posts.author: Joshua (Joshua Park)
posts.cookedHtml: This would be a massive deal for us! I am currently working on a board to calculate the cost of a complete project. The board has a lot of Numbers Columns and Function Columns and it is super important for users to know exactly, which Columns are to fill and which just show resulting data.

posts.author: MONDAYUSER (RANDALL)
posts.cookedHtml:
This feature is a no-brainer. So much easier to visualize related data via color.

I would expect

  1. Ability to set background color for columns.
  2. Ability to set background color for rows.
  3. Ability to easily set alternating rows for color.
  4. Ability to set color by cell.
  5. Ability to set column heading color.
  6. Ability to toggle column/row/cell colors on/off. Maybe in filter menu. Exclude from label columns.
    The color would be a background color so color coded cells like “Labels” would rest on top of the background color.

I would love to see

  1. Ability to change color of row or column based on trigger.
    I think that would be really useful. For example, if time passed on a deadline field then the whole row for that record would change red. That would be cool.
  2. Ability to change color of column based on where the data is from.
    Linked data columns could each be color coded to the board they originally came from. The linked columns could then be laid out on the new board in a way that makes most sense for the data use in that board, but still maintain an easy way to visualize where all the data came from.
  3. Ability to flash color.
    Combined with a trigger, the row would flash color. Settings could adjust flash rate, flash acceleration, color change. For example, a row could be set to flash red when a deadline has passed. A row could be set to flash red faster as a deadline approaches. A row could be set to flash steady (or faster) and change from yellow to red as a deadline approaches. Another example, the row could change color based on an amount in another field going up or down (stock ticker). Or, the row could flash based on the probabilistic outcome of another formula in the table (WARNING - WARNING failure imminent). The row could be part of an integration with a dialer to bring up a record (row) and flash when the call connects or is incoming.

I strongly believe that adding this kind of basic functionality will really inspire the creativity of your partners, developers and user base. We will all be amazed at what Monday can do next, with this tiny tweak now.

posts.author: gordonlangley (Gordon)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes! That would be cool!

posts.author: Topio (Javier Silva)
posts.cookedHtml: No movement on this request?

posts.author: Ari (Ariovaldo Mário dos Santos)
posts.cookedHtml: Totally agree! The tool is fantastic and such features would help us gain productivity!

posts.author: TurnerAndWalker (Luke)
posts.cookedHtml: Wouldn’t hold your breath, requested exactly this ( with alot of support) about 2 years ago, still nothing, it’s a basic function at best and would be invaluable for accounting boards and visual ease of use

posts.author: SRudorf (Sandra Rudorf)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi there, I am also waiting for such a feature. Hope if the community shows increasing interest, something might move in the right direction…

posts.author: Taltsahi (Tsahi Tal)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m still in a bit of a shock that this feature doesn’t exist.
It’s not like it wasn’t in excel for a decade or so and still a very valuable tool for design order.

I hope we get the option to change shading/colors of columns soon… haven’t seen any change regarding this during the last 3 years that I’ve been using Monday

posts.author: Shadster86 (Shadie)
posts.cookedHtml: This would be very valuable, please implement!

posts.author: IngridSantos (Ingrid Santos)
posts.cookedHtml: And again, please Monday DEVs this isn’t a impossible request.

posts.cookedHtml: Another use case for me would be for a schedule change request board I built. I’d like to color code the columns that represent the current schedule, and color the requested schedule columns in another color so that they’re just easier to interpret.

posts.author: MJ-AIFS (MJ)
posts.cookedHtml:
These three items would be top priority for us.
Our company has 50 + boards and this would be a game changer for us.

MONDAYUSER:
  • Ability to set background color for columns.
  • Ability to set background color for rows.
  • Ability to easily set alternating rows for color.

MJ

posts.author: emetzner13 (Evan Metzner)
posts.cookedHtml: Adding my support here, this seems like a fairly simple feature to add and would have a massive impact. I agree with MJ-AIFS above me that setting background color for columns, rows, and alternating rows are the most important and obvious enhancements here. Any feedback on movement of these features would be appreciated.

posts.author: JNewman2020 (Jennifer)
posts.cookedHtml: YES. Why hasn’t this been done yet?!

posts.author: Horizonsong (Karo Zieminski)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree, this is very needed!

posts.author: Allison (Allison Clarke)
posts.cookedHtml:
Would absolutely love these features!

  • We have some rows and columns that apply to certain groups of people, it would be so helpful to colour code so they have a visual on what is relevant to them.
  • Also, for the columns, we would need to be able to colour code by group ie I could see us using colour to highlight the most important columns in one group, which would be different in another group.

posts.author: AKBurczyk (Amanda Burczyk)
posts.cookedHtml:
@CXS This request is years in the making and has massive support. Not sure there is any reason to NOT implement this feature. Allow custom color choices and the ability to re-use colors. would drastically improve usability system wide. @monday-team

We are BEGGING you!

posts.author: mmiller (Michelle Miller)
posts.cookedHtml: This is crazy that nobody from Monday has even replied to this thread.

posts.author: Tabish (Tabish)
posts.cookedHtml:
Looping @Julia-monday.com
This will such an amazing thing if it comes online, without color coding the board looks bland, and it is so easy to get lost if there are too many columns.
@monday-moderators @monday-team @CSG @CXS

posts.author: Orit (Orit)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey everyone, Orit here from monday! I’m a customer experience specialist, working closely with the product team who is in-charge of the board appearance and functionalities.
Happy to e-meet you all!
I read your useful feedback and I will definitely forward it to our team. It’s not planned for now, but I believe the conditional coloring feature is pretty close to what you’re looking for. Have you come across that?
With conditional coloring, you can easily apply coloring to cells that meet your defined criteria to visually emphasize or differentiate among the data on your board. Here you can read more about it.
I hope it helps!

posts.author: stoppa (Gustavo Stoppa Alcantara Santos)
posts.cookedHtml:
@alejencia estava pensando na mesma coisa pois passo pela seguinte situação:
tenho um projeto para ser realizado que possuem duas fases de execução e cada fase possui suas atividades e prazos. Desta forma eu queria agrupar todas as colunas de atividades pertencente a fase A com uma cor e todas as colunas de atividades da base B de outra cor. Isso ajudaria a visualizar melhor o processo ainda mais que no meu caso são pessoas diferentes para executar a fase A e fase B.

Espero que eles entrando que aquela colocaração condicionar não funciona para isso que estamos buscando, que é basicamente uma organização através da cor

posts.author: Bennett (Bennett Killmer)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Orit

Conditional coloring almost works. But because it can’t be saved to the main table, and because very often people arrive at a specific board from the inbox or a notification, which ALWAYS takes them to the main table instead of whatever is set as the default view, conditional coloring might as well not exist

But if that issue could be fixed, I think conditional coloring would be a step toward what I think a lot of us are looking to do.

Thanks for anything you can do to get this resolved!

posts.author: Carson (Carson Butcher)
posts.cookedHtml: Love all your recommendations! Endorse x100!

posts.author: ForTheW (Nathan Erickson)
posts.cookedHtml: Monday is this ever going to happen? Its a no brainer request and a feature set you already support, as conditional coloring exists for cells and rows? Why won’t you institute this?

posts.author: hautey (Heather Autey)
posts.cookedHtml: So what I’m hearing is that Monday.com is not interested in feedback from their users to improve their product. Not cool dev team.

posts.author: ForTheW (Nathan Erickson)
posts.cookedHtml:
Agree with Bennet. The conditional coloring is rendered moot because of the dependencies Monday has set up around default views (main table via notification).

I can’t tell you how much of a UX improvement this would be. Its wild to me it isn’t on the road map. Without color coding, the boards don’t help users work more efficiently.

posts.author: Sara413 (Sara Mahan)
posts.cookedHtml:
Will this ever be an option?

I was shocked when I found out this wasn’t an option - I have 7 teams working off the same board and need to easily be able to identify/update/move columns around based on team needs.

Please get this on the roadmap!

posts.author: Jeremy (Jeremy Rice)
posts.cookedHtml: Has there been any progress on this? It is highly requested by our teams.

posts.author: ForTheW (Nathan Erickson)

Clearly the alternatives aren’t optimal, since so many folks aren’t using it instead. I think Monday should listen to its users and prioritize this issue.

posts.author: Orit (Orit)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey @ForTheW, thank you so much for flagging. Our users’ feedback is super important to us and our product team is well aware of this request and we’re currently assessing the possibility of supporting this request. We will make sure to update you all accordingly.

posts.author: Bill_In_Ohio (Bill Duncan)

With the sheer number of columns I have, this seems very impractical.

posts.author: ForTheW (Nathan Erickson)
posts.cookedHtml: Any updates since July on where this is in Monday’s feasibility assessment?

posts.author: Jorge (Jorge)

posts.author: bgreen (Betsy Green)

posts.author: Danny (DIGITAL STEAM)
posts.cookedHtml:
As a user I would like the ability to use custom colors rather than a limited set of colors;
Pulse groups
Statuses
…
Thanks for considering

posts.author: iamcodymyers (Cody Myers)
posts.cookedHtml:
Agree – would be great to choose custom colors (e.g. brand colors) or be able to use one color multiple times for statuses you use for tracking but don’t necessarily need to notice, per se.

For example, say you have three status columns – 1 for actual status (paused, blocked, in progress, complete, etc.), 1 for priority (Critical, High, Medium, Low, Take your sweet a## time), and maybe 1 for something like visibility (say its a project for internal eyes, external eyes, PR, etc.).

As a visual cue, it’d be nice to set the same light grey for “take your time,” medium, and low priorities, while highlighting the critical and high priority items. In this imaginary use case, maybe visibility isn’t super important its just helpful to track, so having them all be a lighter shade of color to reduce the eyes attention to it when compared to critical priority status column.

Hope that makes sense!

posts.author: Krishele (Krishele Guzman)
posts.cookedHtml:
All in on this! We just spent 2 months greating new brand guidelines and it’d be great to use our new brand colours.

iamcodymyers:

Agree – would be great to choose custom colors (e.g. brand colors)

posts.author: straister (Shelly Traister)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is something I do manually right now across our board types. I do wish that we could actually pick a color more than once, that’s a limiter for us… but visually, we try to use the following as a general rule:
green is good
orange is slippery/alarming
red is bad/stuck
grey is new, or not required
black is closed/cancelled
black is also serving as the color for meetings (which we’re piloting adding to a meetings group on all of our projects and support boards and becomes useful within the context of task types for project tasks.

The push for this is not just the inherent visuals of bad vs. good, but try using the llamas widget to tell you anything intelligent at all besides “wow, that’s a lot of llamas!”. By the color scheme we are using, we are trying to turn all the llama’s green!

This brings me to the bigger issue I face when solutioning in Monday (and heavily relates to the way dasboards combine data from multiple columns/groups/boards). I want black to be closed or cancelled, visually right? but I do need to separate the closed from the cancelled in actual reporting. I can’t do both because I can only use a color once within a column.

If I could use a color twice within a column, and the settings for progress bars/other call that color “complete” or not, no matter how many times I use it, that could work… right Monday!!!

posts.author: Franco (Franco Wiltink)
posts.cookedHtml:
This must not be to difficult to create, make a color picker or let us use the same color multiple times.
How is the status of this request ?

posts.author: andrewmackzum (Andrew Mackzum)
posts.cookedHtml: I’ve been following the development of Monday.com for years-- since they were DaPulse-- and I seriously can’t believe we still can’t use colors more than once. Being able to just reuse the green color multiple times to represent the different ways that column can be “complete” would be a game changer.

posts.author: Matty (Matthew Kinny)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree that we really need the ability to reuse status colours as we are facing the issue of running out of options on a couple of boards and also visually would like different “words” but the same colour.

posts.author: Jasmine (Jasmine Buttolph)
posts.cookedHtml: It would be nice to update the color palette for status and other elements so that when we share the export with clients, it is already in the required branding format. Also - we need to be able to re-use colors or import a gradient of colors so that there could also be a monochromatic color scheme with gradients to show “intensity”. It would be really great to be able to select multiple stock themes (the current default is not color blind friendly) and it would also be great to be able to pick your own color. there could be an option in the color palette that was a color picker that allowed you to add/store new colors.

posts.author: jhulet (Jennifer Hulet)
posts.cookedHtml: Completely agree — custom colors and the ability to choose the same color twice would be AMAZING!

posts.author: alexcamp (Alexandre Campagna)
posts.cookedHtml: Count me in too. Please make a color picker !

posts.author: JRobbins (Joseph Robbins)
posts.cookedHtml: Do we have a timeline for this update by any chance?

posts.author: PrintGeek (Dan Neto)
posts.cookedHtml:
I agree that this feature is of much greater importance to users than it’s being given.

The limited amount of label colors is a big disappointment. Users need to create custom colours through a simple RGB/HEX code picker/input, similar to the already existing “Color Picker” column feature.

Custom colours need to be saved as a master colour and be accessible for all users and all functions so users don’t have to create the same colour across different functions within the account.

The “Red” label colour isn’t even close to red RGB red, and there only one “Red” to choose from. This isn’t acceptable and has led to confusion and productivity issue due to the lack of intuitiveness of the limited colour option that users are forced to use.

We don’t need more colours, we need control over the colours we can create ourselves. Please make this a priority.

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi community, thank you to all those that voted and shared feedback around column color shading. We’ve discussed your request with the development team and they understand why this feature is important to you. As of now, applying color shade to specific columns is not in our upcoming roadmap.

Every quarter, we need to make difficult decisions about how we prioritize building new features, improving old ones, and ensuring overall platform quality.We’re currently focused on improving other key areas of our platform to bring the most impact to the most customers.

Requests like yours help guide us in prioritizing features that our customers care about most, so keep voting and sharing ideas because your feedback makes a difference.

Have another idea? Submit it here.

posts.author: kkovriga (Katerina Kovriga)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi guys,

Our company Stiltsoft develops the Smart Spreadsheet for monday app - hope it will help you to use Excel-like features right on monday.com platform.

You’ll be able to import/export your data (and boards with items as well) and work with it as a fully functional spreadsheet (cell formulas, conditional formatting, pivot tables, etc.) in one place.

Best regards,
Katerina Kovriga

posts.author: DStout (Derek Stout)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, the ability to add color to the headers and columns would allow the visual grouping of columns together. We’re putting together a very detailed accounting and financial month-end close checklist, with several columns for the Preparer Names, Status, Due/Completed Dates, plus the same for Reviewer 1 and Reviewer 2. The ability to group these columns together by color would create a much more professional board, plus allow the users to focus on their columns. This should be added.

posts.author: averyverya (Avery)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 for coloring columns to let team know what columns are automations and which ones arent. Kind of cant believe this isnt just a default feature of Monday!

posts.author: JSMaxwell (Jason Maxwell)
posts.cookedHtml:
As a project manager user of monday.com I ned to be able to color code/shade columns so that I can easily identify related groups of columns.

In more detail: If the first 3 columns are related to step 1 in a workflow, the next 3 columns are related to step 2 in the workflow, the next 3 columns are about step 3, etc, etc, it would be very useful to be able to shade/color the top textbox of each column (where the column name is) so that we can easily visually distinguish the groups.

posts.author: hollyvera (Holly Duque)
posts.cookedHtml: Could everyone also please help me out and vote on this feature (it will benefit everyone greatly for calendar view) Coloring of Columns

posts.author: TanyaB (Tanya B)
posts.cookedHtml: Definately would like to see this

posts.author: Julia1 (Julia)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all, I was looking for something similar as well and came across this post. Do you know if there is any third party app that could help with that?
Thank you

posts.author: kris1 (Kristofer)
posts.cookedHtml: It has been 4 years since this feature was requested. I don’t understand how such a basic and very popular change has not yet been implemented.

posts.author: Fentor (Julian L)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 Would also love to see some kind of feature, even grouping collumns would help.

posts.author: bryceivie (Bryce Ivie)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all. First time posting here, but I think I may have found some sort of solution for column color shading. By using the “conditional coloring” feature at the top of the board’s menu, you can set parameters that fill in every column. For example, I used the following conditions: Cell > Column > is not empty ; Cell > Column > is empty. This color shaded the entire column for me. Obviously, this will become a long list of conditions, but this view can be saved as the default board. Hopefully this helps some of y’all. Take care!

Support

Conditional Coloring

3 min read Feature   With conditional coloring, you can easily apply coloring to cells that meet your defined criteria to visually emphasize or differentiate among the data on your board. This ...

posts.author: Binatica (Binatica)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi folks!
Introducing Color Columns: Transform Your Monday Boards with Vibrant Visuals!

After 4 long years (!!) of anticipation, we are thrilled to introduce Color Columns, the revolutionary app that will give you the capability to color your columns.
Your wait is finally over.

Download now: https://monday.com/marketplace/10000308

Mark Columns: Customize your boards with ease by marking specific columns as automation columns or any other logic that suits your workflow.

Enhanced Readability: No more boring boards! Color Columns adds a vibrant splash of color to your Monday.com boards, making data and tasks easier to digest. It’s not just pretty; it’s practical! Boost team collaboration and overall productivity effortlessly.

Column Clustering: Simplify complex projects and bring clarity to your boards by organizing and grouping relevant columns with color-coded clusters. Finally, you can navigate your workspace with ease.

Data Tracking Made Easy: Keep tabs on progress, deadlines, and milestones effortlessly with our user-friendly color shading system. Never miss a beat and keep everyone in sync with a quick glance.

Productivity Amplified: Say hello to an intuitive and visually appealing interface that will supercharge your team’s productivity. With Color Columns, you can focus on achieving your goals faster and with greater efficiency.

You can take a look at our website: Binatica - Color Columns

Let’s paint a brighter, more efficient future for your team together!
#ColorColumns #MondayMagic #ProductivityBoost

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Everyone,

Update here! After looking further into this request, our product team is exploring different options to develop column color shading and are considering developing it n the upcoming months. I will let you know of any updates.

To see more about what it means when a feature is under consideration., read this post.

posts.author: ProLifePat (Sir Patrick Harrell)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes, this Conditional Coloring solution works, if you apply it to only a few columns.
Once you have ~12 Columns colored, the resulting 24 filter calculations will slow the app.
And, yes, our users wanted 12 columns colored as three colors in groups of four columns.

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml: This feature has gone from “under consideration” to "on roadmap". After discussing this further and reading your feedback our boards team wants to create more robust opportunities. To start, our team is first working with the border column color shading but are also are interested in seeing more use cases from our community to be able to better understand how you would use these capabilities. So please share with your thoughts with us below and make sure you have your notifications turned on for this topic

posts.author: norbert.klein (Norbert Klein)
posts.cookedHtml:
I got to this post because I was using monday and wished that I could do this to one of my mirror columns and did a quick search. I have sub task time tracking mirrored up to my tasks. Next to that column is time tracking for the overall task. for this use case I would like to color the mirror columns, so users know that they are view only.
Thanks for adding to the roadmap.

posts.author: Ozi (Oz Israel)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Julietteb ,

We have a large shareable board with many columns, that only few of them needs to be filled by the board’s geusts. Highlighting these specific columns by color will help orientate.

Thank you,
Oz

posts.author: Katelynn (Katelynn Zuiderveen)
posts.cookedHtml: I would use column coloring for the columns that are connected to a form response.

posts.author: rbeckwith (Rachel)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes I would love to be able to add color conditioning to my mirror columns on a board. That would be amazing!

posts.author: parkwest (Vincent Shonia)
posts.cookedHtml: This should have been a common feature from the get go, considering all excel users use column shading for a wide range of uses. I don’t even know why were having this discussion let alone vote for this.

posts.author: MichaelMutz (Michael Mutz)
posts.cookedHtml: I hope this thing actually comes out. It is hard to keep track of Boards with lots of columns if you want to quickly scroll to different sections of information. At the moment, I typically just stick in “Rating” columns as breakers to help divide the information up to be easier to read. It would just be so much sweeter to simply shade the columns.

posts.author: MichaelMutz (Michael Mutz)
posts.cookedHtml: Also, this has been on the roadmap for 5 years… is it actually being developed?

posts.author: around (Allison Round)
posts.cookedHtml: Adding my name to the list! Was just looking for this feature to implement on one of my boards.

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone! I wanted to update you that I recently checked in with the relevant product team and they let me know that column color shading is currently in development! Since this feature is part of a cluster of updates coming to boards in 2024, it may take a few months till it is ready to be released.

As soon as I know more details about this feature I will update this thread. If you want to stay update with the status of this feature, make sure to turn on notifications for this topic!

How to turn on notifications for a topics Announcements
Hi Everyone! I wanted to share some tips for recieving notificaitons about a specific post. Notifications are especially helpful to stay updates about discussions around feature requests and their statues. To turn on notifications for a specific post, follow these steps: #1: Go to the category of your choice and open the topic you want to receive notifications for #2 Click on the bell on the bottom right of the thread (shown below) #3 Choose from one of the following notification levels: …

Thanks for all the great feedback. It truly helps guide our product team as they develop new features!

@around @MichaelMutz @parkwest @rbeckwith @Katelynn @Ozi @norbert.klein @ProLifePat @bryceivie @Fentor

posts.author: RobbenR (RobbenR)
posts.cookedHtml:
Any update on the release, already 6 months in 2024.

Monday is great, but it frustrates me that I have to subscribe to a marketplace app to get this functionality. (like so many other things)

so any update would be nice.

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Everyone! Status Update: We do not have a definite development timeline for column color shading due to other priorities and team capacity. Our boards team is currently focusing on finishing the development of other highly requested board features, such as the ability to reorder views, wrap text, and undo capabilities. Once these features are completed and released, our team will be able to continue to develop this feature.

In the meantime, there are some great apps that can help you with this. You can look through the thread for suggested apps from our community.

posts.author: DipaliPatankar (Dipali Patankar)
posts.cookedHtml: Looking at the number of votes, likes and comments, I hope this feature will be implemented soon. This will greatly help users transition from Excel trackers into Monday

posts.author: amybronstein (Amy Bronstein)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone. We’ve heard your requests for column color shading to help visually organize and cluster related data. While we’re exploring this as part of our longer-term vision for the platform, it won’t be in active development this year.

Our team remains focused on delivering other highly requested and critical features in the near term, but we’ll continue to evaluate this as we plan for the future. We will continue to share updates as they come!

posts.author: asplundsoftware (Brad)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all

My app, Grouped Columns Table, is currently available in early access for those who want to start trying this out! I think it will help at least in part with the color shading that this thread would like.

The focus is mostly towards grouping columns together of course, but it’s still in the early stages so I’m still flexible with the main feature set. Primarily it is for grouping and coloring columns, but I’d be interested to know how it actually works with your use cases.

https://auth.monday.com/oauth2/authorize?client_id=00a500ab5a438603da3f7a02530cf9c6&response_type=install

If you want in, and to be one of the first to try out our Grouped Columns app, please fill in your info in the Early Access Form: monday.com: Where Teams Get Work Done
so that I can grant your account access to the app.

Some of the features to make your complex-board-viewing life easier:

  1. Ability to add columns to second level column groups
  2. Ability to color columns (entire column, or only the column or group headers!)
  3. Double click to hide columns!
  4. You can either separately manage your column groupings globally, OR individually within each item grouping

Cheers!
Brad

Here is a short demo of grouping, coloring, and collapsing columns:

posts.author: RobbenR (RobbenR)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Monday crew,**

To be honest — you’re encouraging us to use Monday more and more, and we’re doing just that.
But as our boards grow, so do the number of columns — to get the most out of all the great features like views, dashboards, and automations. My main board is getting huge

I know it’s not a high-priority item, but I find it frustrating that I need to purchase an add-on from the Marketplace just to get basic usability improvements. This feels like something that should either be built into the column itself, or supported via extended conditional formatting options.

It’s a small thing, but it would make a big difference in day-to-day usability.

Thanks,
Rick

posts.author: cfriedenberger (Cassandera Friedenberger)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree completely! This is a simple request that we shouldn’t have to pay an additional fee for!

posts.author: asplundsoftware (Brad)
posts.cookedHtml:
I know it’s not a built in feature, which I can see is definitely frustrating and painful for many folks, but if it adds any value here I wanted to share that Column Groups was just released and the aim is to solve this pain directly. It primarily is for grouping columns, but a core feature is coloring columns too.

For those of you curious to try it out, you can find it in the marketplace here:

monday.com

Column Groups - monday.com Marketplace

Simplify your boards - group, color & collapse columns

I would genuinely like to know if it’s helpful for some teams, and what could be improved. Please try it out and reach out to me with your feedback.

Thanks!
Brad
